Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Deem Plaintiffs Unanswered Requests For Admission As Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Heidi Quan, Esq. 415-524-4300 of Murchison & Cumming LLP for Defendant/moving party.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STOP HOME SERVICE ROOFING CO., INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Deem Plaintiffs Unanswered Requests For Admission As Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff.

The unopposed motion of Defendant S'TOP HOME ROOFING SERVICE CO., INC. for an order that Defendant's unanswered Requests for Admission, Set One, by deemed admitted, and for discovery sanctions, is granted. Plaintiff is deemed to have admitted each Request for Admission (1 through 6) set forth in the Requests. Plaintiff is ordered to pay Defendant discovery sanctions in the amount of $546, within thirty days of entry of this order.

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Heidi Quan, Esq. 415-524-4300 of Murchison & Cumming LLP for Defendant/moving party.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STOP HOME SERVICE ROOFING CO., INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

The unopposed motion of Defendant S'TOP HOME ROOFING SERVICE CO., INC. for an order compelling plaintiff PAMELA WHITEHEAD to respond to Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, served August 7, 2017, and for discovery sanctions, is granted. Plaintiff is to serve code-compliant responses, without objections, and pay Defendant discovery sanctions in the amount of $560, within thirty days of entry of this order.

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)
